User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the PXL8 DTF Platform.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

createdAt.description: Date and time the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Date and time the user account was last updated.

GangSheet.title: GangSheet
GangSheet.description: Represents a gang sheet created by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GangSheet entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who created the gang sheet. (Relationship: User 1:N GangSheet)

imageUrl.description: URL of the gang sheet image stored in Firebase Storage.

width.description: Width of the gang sheet in inches.

height.description: Height of the gang sheet in inches.

dpi.description: DPI (dots per inch) of the gang sheet.

createdAt.description: Date and time the gang sheet was created.

updatedAt.description: Date and time the gang sheet was last updated.

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents an order placed by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who placed the order. (Relationship: User 1:N Order)

gangSheetIds.description: References to the GangSheets included in the order. (Relationship: Order 1:N GangSheet)

status.description: Current status of the order (e.g., Pending, Processing, Shipped, Delivered).

createdAt.description: Date and time the order was created.

updatedAt.description: Date and time the order was last updated.

SheetSize.title: SheetSize
SheetSize.description: Represents a pricing tier for a gang sheet size.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SheetSize entity.

name.description: Display name for the sheet size (e.g., Small, 22x24).

width.description: Width of the sheet in inches.

height.description: Height of the sheet in inches.

price.description: Price for this sheet size in USD.

usage.description: Determines where this pricing tier is available.

definition.description: Stores user profile information.  Path-based ownership for private user data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

definition.description: Stores gang sheets created by users. Path-based ownership ensures only the user can access their gang sheets.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user who created the gang sheet.

params.name: gangSheetId
params.description: The unique identifier for the gang sheet.

definition.description: Stores orders placed by users. Path-based ownership ensures only the user can access their orders.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user who placed the order.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier for the order.

definition.description: Stores global pricing tiers for gang sheet sizes. Writable only by admins.

params.name: sheetSizeId
params.description: The unique identifier for the sheet size.

firestore.reasoning: The Firestore structure is designed to store user data, gang sheets, and orders in a way that prioritizes security, scalability, and ease of debugging. Authorization Independence is achieved by leveraging path-based ownership for gang sheets and orders, eliminating the need for complex `get()` calls in security rules. All `list` operations are secured by the path-based ownership, ensuring that users can only access their own data. This also adheres to the principle that "Rules are not Filters" (QAPs). The structure uses explicit state modeling via the 'status' field in the Order entity. Furthermore, the data model enforces radical consistency by utilizing semantic naming conventions for collections, documents, and fields (e.g., always `userId` for user references). The use of hierarchical paths for User-owned entities like GangSheets (`/users/{userId}/gangSheets/{gangSheetId}`) and Orders (`/users/{userId}/orders/{orderId}`) ensures that ownership-based security rules are simple and efficient. This structure facilitates secure `list` operations since rules can simply check `request.auth.uid == userId`. The new `/sheetSizes` collection is a global, root-level collection for pricing information, readable by all authenticated users but writable only by admins.
